metadata.title: Build a Brand Palette Accessibility Auditor with Claude
metadata.note: cli-ideas Card 21 — Ch 9 (Visual Identity Systems). DOUBLE-CHECK: WCAG 2.2 AA thresholds: normal text 4.5:1, large text/graphical objects 3:1. Formula: L = 0.2126R + 0.7152G + 0.0722B on linearized channels; ratio = (L_lighter + 0.05)/(L_darker + 0.05). warm-clay #A89068 on white #FFFFFF: ratio ≈ 3.1:1 — FAIL for normal text, PASS for AA-large. Ch 9 example: caption text #9CA3AF on #FAFAF8 fails at 2.9:1, fix: darken to #6B7280 → 4.6:1. Corrected alternative for warm-clay: near #7A6045 achieving ≥4.5:1.

beats.role_note: COLD OPEN LAW — Claude UI, ask shown answered; IN-FOR-BEAR
beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Build it with Claude, then take it apart.

props.command: claude "run WCAG contrast audit on this brand palette — compute ratios, flag FAIL, propose corrected hex"
output: warm-clay #A89068 on white: ratio 3.1:1 — FAIL normal, PASS large
3/6 pairings pass AA for normal text

beats.role_note: stakes — BEFORE CLI loop; SHOW-DON'T-TELL: animate the contrast ratio bars
beats.narration_text: A palette that fails WCAG AA cannot be read by some fraction of your audience. A color combination below four-point-five to one contrast ratio for normal text is not a stylistic choice — it is a documented barrier to access, and shipping it signals you did not think carefully about who you designed for. WCAG 2.2 AA is also a legal requirement in many jurisdictions for public-facing digital products. The correction is almost always minor: darkening or lightening fifteen to twenty percent. The chapter's worked example: warm-clay hex A-8-9-0-6-8 on white comes in at three-point-one to one — FAIL for normal text, PASS for large text only. Darken to near 7-A-6-0-4-5 and the ratio crosses four-point-five. The palette character does not change. The implementation is now compliant.
beats.visual_intent: Animated horizontal bar chart: contrast ratios for 6 palette combinations — bars growing to each ratio; AA reference lines at 4.5:1 (normal text) and 3:1 (large text); warm-clay bar stops at 3.1:1, arrow pointing to 'FAIL normal — fix needed'; corrected bar animates to 4.8:1 crossing the 4.5 line; no composer yet

beats.role_note: ClaudeComposerAsk — show and discuss the prompt
beats.narration_text: Into Claude. Given palette hex codes and intended text-on-background pairings, compute the WCAG contrast ratios. The formula: relative luminance L equals 0.2126 times R plus 0.7152 times G plus 0.0722 times B, on linearized channels. Contrast ratio equals L-lighter plus 0.05 divided by L-darker plus 0.05. Rate each pairing: PASS-AAA at seven to one or above, PASS-AA-normal at four-point-five to one, PASS-AA-large at three to one for eighteen-point-plus text, or FAIL. For every FAIL: nearest hex in the same hue family achieving AA, with the corrected ratio shown. Output: table of pairing, ratio, rating, and fix-if-fail — then a summary count of pairings passing AA for normal text.

props.command:
claude "WCAG 2.2 AA contrast audit for palette:
  [HEX CODES WITH LABELS]
  Test pairings: [TEXT HEX] on [BACKGROUND HEX]
  For each:
  1. Relative luminance: L = 0.2126R + 0.7152G + 0.0722B
     (linearize: x/255 ≤0.04045→x/12.92, else ((x+0.055)/1.055)^2.4)
  2. Ratio = (L_lighter+0.05)/(L_darker+0.05)
  3. Rate: PASS-AAA(≥7) | PASS-AA-normal(≥4.5) |
            PASS-AA-large(≥3) | FAIL
  4. For FAIL: nearest hex same hue → AA + corrected ratio
  Output: table + summary N/M pairings pass AA normal"

beats.role_note: ClaudeCodeBeat — actual contrast auditor script; trimmed to the lines that teach
beats.narration_text: Read it. The luminance calculation is the critical path. The linearization step is non-obvious — channel values above 0.04045 follow a gamma curve, not a linear one; getting this wrong shifts the ratio by a measurable amount. The correction logic stays in the same hue family: adjust value only, not hue, so the palette character survives the fix. That is the design constraint built into the code.

props.title: contrast_audit.py

beats.role_note: screen-rec or animated slot — contrast ratio table with FAIL rows highlighted
beats.narration_text: Run it on a warm archetype palette including warm-clay A-8-9-0-6-8 on white. Table prints: six pairings, ratios computed. Warm-clay on white: 3.1 to 1 — FAIL normal, PASS large. Caption text 9-C-A-3-A-F on near-white F-A-F-A-F-8: 2.9 to 1 — FAIL. The corrected alternatives print beside each failing row: warm-clay corrected to 7-A-6-0-4-5 gives 4.8 to 1. Caption corrected to 6-B-7-2-8-0 gives 4.6 to 1. Both stay in the same hue family. Summary: three of six pairings pass AA for normal text.
beats.visual_intent: screen-rec or Manim bar chart: 6 bars — 3 above 4.5:1 line (green), 2 at 3.1 and 2.9:1 below (red FAIL), 1 at 8.9:1 AAA; beside each FAIL: arrow + corrected hex + new ratio crossing the AA line; summary: '3/6 PASS → after fix: 5/6 PASS'

beats.narration_text: Apply the corrected hex values and re-run the audit to verify. Then check the non-text elements — icons, decorative graphics, and UI component boundaries fall under the three-to-one threshold, not four-point-five. Ask the auditor to separate text and non-text pairings and apply the correct threshold to each.

props.command:
claude "re-run WCAG audit with corrected hex values:
  warm-clay corrected: #7A6045
  caption corrected: #6B7280
  Verify all text pairings now pass AA-normal (≥4.5:1)
  Then audit non-text pairings (icons, borders, UI elements)
  Apply 3:1 threshold for non-text.
  Output: updated table with corrected ratios.
  Final count: N/M pairings pass appropriate threshold."

beats.role_note: Manim or screen-rec — corrected palette all pairings passing
beats.narration_text: Re-run. The corrected palette prints five of six text pairings passing AA for normal text. The sixth is a decorative element below 4.5 — it passes the 3 to 1 non-text threshold. All rows green. The palette character is preserved — same hues, same visual identity — and the implementation is now compliant across all intended uses.
beats.visual_intent: Manim bar chart: all 6 bars now at or above their respective thresholds — 5 text bars above 4.5 line, 1 non-text bar above 3.0 line; corrected hex values labeled beside each; annotation 'palette character preserved — value adjusted, hue unchanged'

beats.narration_text: The correction is almost always minor — darkening or lightening fifteen to twenty percent. The failure is always avoidable. A palette that fails contrast is not just legally risky in many jurisdictions. It is a signal about who you designed for. The audit runs in seconds. The brand review that discovers the failure after launch takes considerably longer to recover from.
beats.visual_intent: Summary: WCAG thresholds as a reference card (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large/non-text); before-after palette swatches for warm-clay; bottom: 'WCAG AA is not a recommendation in many jurisdictions — it is a legal requirement'

beats.role_note: HANDOFF LAW — prompt READ aloud AND discussed; greeting 'Your turn.'
beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this prompt with your brand palette hex codes and the text-on-background pairings you actually use in your interface. Read the FAIL rows first — those are the ones blocking legal compliance and readability for users with low contrast sensitivity. The corrected hex alternatives will stay in your hue family. Apply them and re-run. The two-pass audit — text pairings first, non-text second — is the complete check. Do not ship a palette that has not passed this audit.

props.command:
claude "WCAG audit for my brand palette:
  Palette: [HEX CODES WITH ROLE LABELS]
  Text pairings: [TEXT on BACKGROUND list]
  Non-text pairings: [ICON/BORDER on BACKGROUND list]
  Compute ratios, rate against 4.5:1 (text) and 3:1 (non-text)
  For every FAIL: nearest corrected hex same hue → AA
  Final: how many pairings pass before and after corrections"

beats.narration_text: Build a Brand Palette Accessibility Auditor with Claude. This is Liam, in for Bear — build it with Claude, then take it apart.

props.title: Build a Brand Palette Accessibility Auditor.
